heaviest_rainfall.status: 47.89mm of rain was recorded as having fallen on 09 November 2025, marking an exceptional 144.8 hour precipitation event.  At times the rain was falling at 24.6mm per hour.

heaviest_rainrate.status: 34.3mm per hour is the maximum rain rate ever recorded on 02 September 2025, during a 15.0 hour storm which dumped 21.19mm of rain from the clouds.

longest_rain.status: The record duration for continuous rain was set on 09 November 2025, the storm lasted 144.8 hours with a total precipitation of 47.89mm and it reached 24.6mm per hour at times.

most_lightning.status: The most lightning strikes in one day since 11 April 2025 is 23 and it happened on 07 September 2025.

storm.status: Stormy conditions are expected.  This is the first storm in the region since 21 December 2025.

top_10_dry.status: The ongoing dry stretch, 9 days and counting started on 23 December 2025 and ranks 7th in length since records started in 23 September 2024.  The longest dry period started on 24 April 2025 and lasted for 19 days.

top_10_rainy.status: The longest rainy period started on 26 November 2025 and lasted for 14 days.

cold_snaps.status: There is currently no cold snap.  This year there have been 6 cold snaps, usually there are 3.7 cold snaps per year.

extreme.status: The largest temperature difference recorded was 26.7°C on 29 April 2025.  On that date the temperature reached 27.1°C and fell to 0.4°C.

heatwaves.status: At the moment, it is not a heatwaves.  This year has seen 6 heatwavess; typically, 1.9 heatwavess happen every year.

tropical.status: The previous tropical night was on 18 July 2025, with only 62 in total since 1973.

average.status: The current average daily wind direction is southerly (198°).  In the UK, a southerly wind usually brings milder, moist air from the Atlantic.

gusts.status: To date, the maximum wind gust recorded is 93.7 mph on 13 October 2012.

run.status: 1737 miles is the largest wind run recorded on 21 October 2012.

speed.status: 93.7 mph is the largest wind speed recorded on 13 October 2012.

top_10_windy.status: The longest windy period started on 11 October 2012 and lasted for 21 days.

summary.description:
Today, cooler for the inside temperature (down 7.7°C), outside temperature (down 4.7°C), and feels like temperature (down 3.8°C).

Compared to today, yesterday also had noticeable differences in cloud levels (up 295 m).

The past week and month had observable variation in dew point temperature (up 8.5°C), outside temperature (up 8.7°C), cloud levels (up 340 m), and inside humidity (up 7%) when compared to today.

cloud levels.description:
Today’s average cloud levels is 294.6m lower than yesterday, 340.0m lower than the past week, 24.1m higher than the past month.

Yesterday, the cloud levels ranged from a minimum of 828m to a maximum of 2280m, averaging 986.3m; the past week and month had slightly higher averages of 1326.3m and 962.2m respectively.

summary.summary: Inside; comfortable and pleasant temperatures. Outside; subzero temperatures are forecasted. Also, the atmosphere will feel parched and dry and winds will be practically absent. Drizzle or light rain possible, clouds will occasionally block sunlight, and periods of sunshine are expected. High pressure will dominate, leading to stable weather.

washing.phrase: The current temperature is too low (-2.2°C) for optimal drying conditions.  Any drying will take longer.
